---
name: review
description: 'Autonomous PR review that posts high-signal, human-voiced comments under your account. Use when reviewing someone else''s PR (or your own manifest-driven PR) and you want a precision-tuned review you can walk away from. Triggers: review pr, autonomous review, post pr review, autoreview, loop review, watch pr review.'
argument-hint: '[pr-url] [--manifest <path>] [--bundle <urls>] [--loop]'
user-invocable: true
---

**Resolve inputs.** Take `pr-url` from the arg or infer from the current branch's upstream PR; no PR → abort with a reason. Auto-discover the manifest from `.manifest/` only when the PR's head branch is yours; `--manifest <path>` overrides. Parse linked PRs from the PR description and conversation (`Depends on #N`, `Stack:`, `Co-changes:`, PR URLs); `--bundle <urls>` merges in; cap 5.

**Spawn the fleet in parallel.** Always-on: `change-intent-reviewer`, `code-bugs-reviewer`, `code-design-reviewer`, `code-maintainability-reviewer`, `code-simplicity-reviewer`, `context-file-adherence-reviewer`. Content-gated when the diff fits: `type-safety-reviewer` on typed code; `test-quality-reviewer` and `code-testability-reviewer` on source; `contracts-reviewer` on API surfaces; `operational-readiness-reviewer` on CI/infra/env/migrations/workers/queues/secrets; `docs-reviewer` and `prose-value-reviewer` on prose; `prompt-reviewer` (with `prompt-token-efficiency-verifier`, `prompt-compression-verifier`) on prompts/skills/agents. Forward the manifest to every spawned reviewer when present. **Reviewers stay narrow-lens — never pass them PR conversation or bundle context.** Filter aggregated findings to Medium+ (drop Low).

**Holistic coherence pass.** Spawn one subagent with the Medium+ findings + PR history (most recent ~50 review/conversation comments, all unresolved threads regardless of recency, the author's last 5 commit messages on the branch, the PR description) + bundle context (≤5 linked PRs: diff, description, top-level conversation — no inline review comments from bundle PRs) + the manifest if present. Carry forward any truncation the caller did (e.g., PR had >50 comments, bundle PR inaccessible). The subagent (a) **prunes** findings already raised by another reviewer in the existing PR conversation, already discussed/conceded by the author, contradicting manifest intent, contradicted by the author's prior explanation in a comment / commit message / PR-description note, or piling on an active in-progress thread; (b) **dedupes across reviewers** — merge into one comment when same file + overlapping line range + same underlying concern; (c) **anchors** each surviving finding to exactly one of inline file:line (default), file-level (whole-file concern), PR-level (cross-cutting concern with no specific anchor); (d) **rewrites every comment body, summary header text, and any drafted reply in the voice profile below**; (e) **omits a summary header by default** — adds one only when there's a real overall take the per-comment list misses (one short sentence, voice-compliant, no boilerplate). Returns: comments to post (anchor + voice-compliant body), summary header text if any, truncation notes, a brief dropped-findings tally with dominant reasons.

**Voice profile.** Each comment, header, or thread reply is one thought: state the problem, point to evidence inline (file:line, short code excerpt when load-bearing), suggest the fix. Direct, concrete, no softeners. **Never** in any posted body: severity labels (`[High]`, `⚠️`, `Critical:`), markdown headers or bold-the-takeaway in single-thought comments, emoji of any kind, em-dash rhetorical flourishes (especially "It's not just X — it's Y" / "not just A, but B"), bulleted recommendation lists for a single suggestion, softeners ("I think", "I recommend", "It seems", "Perhaps consider"), opener boilerplate ("Great PR!", "Nice change, but..."), "at the location above" / "as mentioned" (always name file:line inline), AI disclosure footer — no flag for one, ever. Target voice example: *"Empty input skips the null check — `if (input?.value)` at `parser.ts:42` short-circuits before the parse at `parser.ts:47`, so `{}` reaches `parse()` without the guard. Tighten to `if (input?.value != null)`, or move the `parse()` call inside the existing branch."*

**Plan and post.** Present the holistic pass output via ExitPlanMode (comments + anchors, context loaded, any truncation, dropped-finding tally). On approval, submit a single GitHub PR review with decision `comment` (never `request_changes`, never `approve` here) — all comments batched atomically. **Zero Medium+ findings:** ask via AskUserQuestion — `"Looks good to me — post as approval on the PR?"`. Approve → submit decision `approve` with body `Looks good to me.`; decline → exit silent. No PR action otherwise.

**`--loop`.** Load `references/LOOP.md`.
